title: macOS Swift exec allowlist parser missed combined POSIX inline-command flags
description: extractPosixInlineCommand() in apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ExecShellWrapperParser.swift extracts the inline shell command payload that the macOS exec allowlist evaluates. It read only the single token at command[1] and required it to be an exact member of posixInlineFlags ({"-lc", "-c", "--command"}), then took the payload from command[2]. A command request that expresses the inline-command flag in a combined POSIX form (for example -ic, -lic, or other clustered short flags ending in c) does not match any allowlist flag, so extractPayload() returns nil and the invocation is treated as not a shell wrapper, causing the inline shell content to skip the allowlist check and run outside the intended allowlist.
